- Be cautious of outdated or incorrect information, especially in historical contexts.

Action Step 2.sub_goal: Identify the president who warned about the military-industrial complex.

result:
The president who warned about the military-industrial complex was Dwight D. Eisenhower. He delivered this warning in his farewell address on January 17, 1961[1](https://eisenhowerfoundation.net/primary-source/president-eisenhower-warns-military-industrial-complex-farewell-address), [2](https://www.ebsco.com/research-starters/history/eisenhower-warns-military-industrial-complex), [3](https://www.archives.gov/milestone-documents/president-dwight-d-eisenhowers-farewell-address).

Dwight D. Eisenhower died on March 28, 1969[4](https://www.thenmusa.org/biographies/dwight-david-eisenhower/), [5](https://www.airlant.usff.navy.mil/Organization/Aircraft-Carriers/USS-Dwight-D-Eisenhower-CVN-69/Namesake-Dwight-D-Eisenhower/), [6](https://info.mysticstamp.com/this-day-in-history-march-28-1969_tdih/), [7](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dwight_D._Eisenhower), [8](https://millercenter.org/president/eisenhower).
